On the Clock 是 Motherboard 对有组织的劳工运动、零工工作、自动化和工作的未来的报告。根据美国国家劳工关系委员会的指控和其他文件，亚马逊非法禁止员工向工人提供支持工会的文献，没收了该文献，并让员工觉得他们的组织活动在该公司位于纽约的史坦顿岛运营中心受到监视主板审核。 NLRB 的一项调查发现，5 月 16 日，亚马逊非法禁止参与工会组织的史坦顿岛员工康纳·斯宾塞 (Connor Spence) 在休息室分发支持工会的文献——然后没收了这些文献——这也违反了美国劳动法。 NLRB 向工会律师提供的证据。 Connor Spence 是史坦顿岛亚马逊 JFK8 配送中心的一名 25 岁仓库工人，他提出了不公平劳动行为指控，他告诉主板，5 月 16 日，他在休息室分发有关工会的传单和通知副本亚马逊因侵犯工人工会权利而不得不在皇后区的一个仓库张贴，当时一名亚马逊保安走近他并告诉他，他无权分发传单。 “他拿走了工会文献，不会还给，”斯宾塞告诉主板。 “我提出了指控，以便追究责任，防止他们将来这样做。”今年 4 月，在阿拉巴马州贝塞默 (Bessemer) 的一个仓库举行的备受瞩目的工会运动失败后，史坦顿岛的亚马逊仓库工人一直忙于组织自己的独立工会，即亚马逊工会。 “亚马逊非常明显地反对工会。在阻止工人加入工会方面，他们经常越界，”斯宾塞说。 “不幸的是，我们国家的劳动法不是很严格，但我希望亚马逊关心自己的形象和记录上的这些污点。”
同一天，阿拉巴马州的一名 NLRB 官员发布了一份报告，建议在阿拉巴马州贝塞默的亚马逊仓库重新举行工会选举。 Bessemer 活动标志着迄今为止在美国建立亚马逊仓库工会的最引人注目的努力，并激励全国各地的亚马逊工人团体采取措施加入工会。 NLRB 关于 Bessemer 选举的报告发现，亚马逊非法阻止劳工组织，部分是通过推动邮局官员在仓库外安装一个邮箱，在那里敦促工人放下邮寄选票，一名 NLRB 官员写道“摧毁了实验室条件并证明第二次选举是合理的。”最近几个月，亚马逊在其史坦顿岛仓库传播了类似于贝塞默工会运动中使用的反工会信息，短信内容为“不要被穿着亚马逊背心的工会组织者误导”、浴室标牌、整个监控器它的仓库。 NLRB 的调查还发现，亚马逊在 5 月 24 日非法制造了对 JFK8 工人组织活动进行监视的印象。 大多数星期，大批工人聚集在仓库外的人行道上进行工会烧烤，其中包括热狗、汉堡包、烤玉米饼、和海鲜通心粉和奶酪。斯宾塞说，5 月 24 日，一群亲工会的工人正在几个帐篷下举行其中一个烧烤，这时一名保安从附近的停车场走近并举起手机给组织者拍照。主板查看了一张保安拍照的照片。根据 1935 年的《国家劳工关系法》，雇主干涉或报复参与工会活动的工人是违法的，包括监视或制造工会组织被监视的印象。在过去的一年里，NLRB 一再发现亚马逊非法违反这些法律的证据。 “违反 [NLRA] 的处罚毫无意义，”旧金山州立大学工会回避行业专家约翰洛根说。 “[对于亚马逊]，这是开展业务的成本。你可以证明亚马逊属于最严重的违法者，是违反劳动法的典型代表。”在 Spence 的案件中，NLRB 已经对指控作出了认定，并表示计划提出正式投诉。对案情的认定不是官方决定，而是正在进行的程序中的关键步骤。亚马逊现在将有机会和解。如果亚马逊不同意这样做，则 NLRB 将安排在行政法官面前举行听证会。
亚马逊没有回应有关 Spence 的指控及其在史坦顿岛的反工会活动的评论请求，但就贝塞默的选举告诉主板，“我们的员工有机会在所有类型的声音都在嘈杂的时间里被听到在全国辩论中权衡利弊，在一天结束时，他们以压倒性多数投票赞成与他们的经理和公司建立直接联系。他们的声音应该高于一切，我们计划呼吁以确保发生这种情况。”
note_en:
On the Clock is Motherboard's reporting on the organized labor movement, gig work, automation, and the future of work.
  Amazon illegally prohibited an employee from giving workers pro-union literature, confiscated that literature, and gave workers the impression that their organizing activity was being surveilled at the company's Staten Island fulfillment center in New York, according to National Labor Relations Board charges and other documentation reviewed by Motherboard.
  An NLRB investigation found that Amazon illegally prohibited Connor Spence, a Staten Island employee involved in union organizing, from distributing pro-union literature in a break room on May 16—and then confiscated the literature—also in violation of U.S. labor law, according to evidence provided by the NLRB to the union’s attorney.
    Connor Spence, a 25-year-old warehouse worker in Amazon's JFK8 fulfillment center in Staten Island, who filed the unfair labor practice charge, told Motherboard that on May 16, he was in the break room distributing leaflets about unions and copies of a notice that Amazon   had to post in a Queens warehouse for violating workers’ union rights, when an Amazon security guard approached him and told him he did not have permission to distribute the leaflets.
  “He took the union literature away and wouldn’t give it back,” Spence told Motherboard. “I filed the charge so that there’s accountability in place that prevents them from doing this in the future.”
  Following the defeat of a   high-profile union drive at a warehouse in Bessemer, Alabama this April, Amazon warehouse workers in Staten Island have   been busy organizing their own independent union, known as Amazon Labor Union.
  “Amazon is very obviously anti-union. They cross the line a lot when it comes to stopping workers from unionizing,” Spence said. “Unfortunately labor law isn’t very strong in our country, but I’m hoping Amazon cares about its image and these stains on their record.”
    The finding comes on the same day as an NLRB officer in Alabama released a report recommending the   rerun of a union election in an Amazon warehouse in Bessemer, Alabama. The Bessemer campaign marked the highest profile effort to date to unionize an Amazon warehouse in the United States, and inspired groups of Amazon workers around the country to take steps toward unionizing. The NLRB’s report on the Bessemer election found that Amazon illegally discouraged labor organizing, in part by pushing post office officials to install a mailbox outside the warehouse where workers were urged to drop their mail-in ballots, which an NLRB officer wrote “destroyed the laboratory conditions and justifies a second election.”
  In recent months, Amazon has spread   anti-union messaging similar to that used in the Bessemer union drive at its Staten Island warehouse with   text messages that said “Don't be misled by union organizers wearing Amazon vests,” signage in bathrooms, monitors throughout its warehouse.
  The NLRB investigation also found that Amazon illegally created the impression of surveillance of workers’ organizing activity at JFK8 on May 24.
    Most weeks, large groups of workers have gathered for union barbecues on the sidewalk outside the warehouse with hotdogs, hamburgers, baked ziti, and seafood mac and cheese. Spence said that on May 24, a group of pro-union workers was holding one of these barbecues under a couple of tents, when a security guard approached from a nearby parking lot and held up her phone to take photos of the organizers. Motherboard reviewed a photo of the security guard taking photos.
   Under the National Labor Relations Act of 1935, it is illegal for employers to interfere with or retaliate against workers involved in union activity, including by surveilling or creating the impression of surveillance of union organizing. During the past year, the NLRB has   repeatedly   found   evidence that Amazon has illegally violated these laws.
  “The penalties for violating the [NLRA] are meaningless,” said John Logan, an expert on the union avoidance industry at San Francisco State University. “[For Amazon], it’s the cost of doing business. You can make a case that Amazon falls into the category of the worst offenders, a poster child for labor law violations.”
  In Spence’s case, the NLRB has made a finding of merit to the charges, and has indicated that it plans to issue a formal complaint. A finding of merit is not an official decision, but a crucial step in an ongoing proceeding. Amazon will now have the opportunity to settle. If Amazon does not agree to do so, then the NLRB will schedule a hearing before an administrative law judge.
  Amazon did not respond to a request for comment about Spence’s charges and its anti-union activity in Staten Island, but told Motherboard regarding the election in Bessemer, “Our employees had a chance to be heard during a noisy time when all types of voices were weighing into the national debate, and at the end of the day, they voted overwhelmingly in favor of a direct connection with their managers and the company. Their voice should be heard above all else, and we plan to appeal to ensure that happens.”
title: 亚马逊非法没收工会文献，NLRB 发现
title_en: Amazon Unlawfully Confiscated Union Literature, NLRB Finds

路透华盛顿 8 月 3 日 - 美国证券交易委员会 (SEC) 主席周二呼吁国会赋予该机构更多权力，以更好地监管加密货币交易、贷款和平台，他说“狂野西部”充满了谜团欺诈和投资者风险。 Gary Gensler 表示，加密市场涉及许多代币，这些代币可能是未注册的证券，价格容易受到操纵，数百万投资者容易受到风险的影响。 “这一资产类别在某些应用程序中充斥着欺诈、诈骗和滥用，”Gensler 在一次全球会议上说。 “我们需要更多的国会授权来防止交易、产品和平台陷入监管漏洞。”随着越来越多的投资者在其投资组合中存入数字代币，加密货币在 4 月份达到了创纪录的 2 万亿美元，但对市场的监督仍然不完整。该行业一直在屏息等待，看看 4 月份接任美国证券交易委员会的民主党任命者 Gensler 将如何对市场进行监督，他此前曾表示应该将其纳入传统的金融监管范围。周二，Gensler 就他的想法提供了更多见解，称他希望国会赋予 SEC 监管加密货币交易的权力，目前这些交易不在 SEC 的职权范围内。他还呼吁立法者赋予美国证券交易委员会更多权力来监督加密贷款，以及允许贷方和借款人在没有传统银行的情况下进行加密货币交易的点对点去中心化金融 (DeFi) 网站等平台。
“如果我们不解决这些问题，我担心很多人会受到伤害，”Gensler 说。民主党参议员伊丽莎白沃伦一直在敦促监管机构控制市场，她在 7 月给 Gensler 的一封信中将其描述为“高度不透明且不稳定”。阅读更多 Gensler 回应要求国会考虑授予他更多的自主权来监管该行业。周二，他还强调“股票代币、由证券支持的稳定价值代币或任何其他提供对基础证券的综合敞口的虚拟产品……都受证券法的约束。”总部位于华盛顿的区块链协会的负责人克里斯汀史密斯表示，虽然加密行业渴望帮助找到解决 SEC 担忧的“可行解决方案”，但它目前确实遵守州当局和其他联邦监管机构的监督。 “该行业与 Gensler 主席的许多目标相同，包括对加密行业进行明智、适当的监管，鼓励法律确定性、稳健的市场完整性以及投资者/客户保护，”史密斯在一份声明中说。 “我们与 Gensler 主席的不同之处在于，他将不断增长的加密经济描述为‘狂野西部’，”史密斯说，“加密行业远非不受监管。”
note_en:
WASHINGTON, Aug 3 (Reuters) - The chair of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Tuesday called on Congress to give the agency more authority to better police cryptocurrency trading, lending and platforms, a "Wild West" he said is riddled with fraud and investor risk.
 Gary Gensler said the crypto market involves many tokens which may be unregistered securities and leaves prices open to manipulation and millions of investors vulnerable to risks.
 "This asset class is rife with fraud, scams and abuse in certain applications," Gensler told a global conference. "We need additional congressional authorities to prevent transactions, products and platforms from falling between regulatory cracks."
 Cryptocurrencies reached a record capitalization of $2 trillion in April as more investors stocked their portfolios with digital tokens, but oversight of the market remains patchy.
 The industry has been waiting with bated breath to see how Gensler, a Democratic appointee who took the SEC helm in April, will approach oversight of the market, which he has previously said should be brought within traditional financial regulation.
 On Tuesday, Gensler provided more insight on his thinking, saying he would like Congress to give the SEC the power to oversee cryptocurrency exchanges, which are not currently within the SEC's remit.
 He also called on lawmakers to give the SEC more power to oversee crypto lending, and platforms like peer-to-peer decentralized finance (DeFi) sites that allow lenders and borrowers to transact in cryptocurrencies without traditional banks.
  "If we don't address these issues, I worry a lot of people will be hurt," Gensler said.
 Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren has been pressing regulators to get a grip on the market, which she described in a July letter to Gensler as "highly opaque and volatile."   read more
 Gensler responded by asking Congress to consider granting him more autonomy to regulate the sector.
 On Tuesday, he also underscored that "stock tokens, a stable value token backed by securities, or any other virtual product that provides synthetic exposure to underlying securities ... are subject to the securities laws."
 Kristin Smith, who runs the Washington-based Blockchain Association said that while the crypto industry is eager to help find "workable solutions" to the SEC's concerns, it does currently comply with oversight by state authorities and other federal regulatory bodies.
 "The industry shares many of Chair Gensler's goals, including smart, appropriate regulation of the crypto industry, encouraging legal certainty, robust market integrity, and investor/customer protection," Smith said in a statement.
 "Where we differ with Chair Gensler is his characterization of the growing crypto economy as the 'Wild West,' Smith said. "The crypto industry is far from unregulated."
title: 美国证券交易委员会主席呼吁国会帮助控制加密货币“狂野西部”
title_en: SEC Chair Calls On Congress To Help Rein In Crypto 'Wild West'

周日，一个由两党组成的参议员小组发布了一项大规模的新的两党基础设施​​法案的草案，提出了超过一万亿美元的支出和一系列影响深远的条款。但该法案中一个鲜为人知的部分可能对打击酒后驾车产生重大影响，最终强制要求采用一种新的车内安全技术来积极防止美国人在酒后驾车。该条款在“高级障碍驾驶技术”标题下引入，将要求交通部制定检测和预防障碍驾驶的新标准。该法案要求交通部长在三年内发布一项标准，三年后该要求对新车生效。该标准的具体规定含糊不清，但会要求汽车“被动监测机动车驾驶人的表现，以准确识别该驾驶人是否可能受到损害”，如果发现损害则“阻止或限制机动车运行” .该系统将“被动地监控驾驶员的表现......以确定该驾驶员是否可能受到损害”创建该系统的具体方法仍未确定，但拥护者表示大部分技术已经可用。雷克萨斯、宝马和梅赛德斯奔驰已经在某些车型中提供了驾驶员监控系统，该系统跟踪驾驶员的面部或眼睑以确保他们保持警觉并积极驾驶车辆。车道检测等系统也可用于检测障碍，如果驾驶员一直在车道外转向，则会发出警报。 “20 年前，这项技术还不存在，”汽车安全中心的 Jason Levine 说。 “[但是]我们现在拥有可用的技术。我们可以在车辆中安装技术，帮助监控某人是否受损并阻止该人伤害自己或他人。”至关重要的是，新标准不仅限于酒驾。由于系统直接测量损伤，因此它们在检测处方药、情绪困扰或简单分心造成的损伤方面同样有效。一项长期努力还将寻求强制使用被动酒精监测系统，例如沃尔沃目前正在开发的系统。虽然这些规定旨在为汽车制造商制定新的强制性要求，但距离这样的要求还有很长的路要走。围绕基础设施法案的谈判仍在进行中，立法者仍可取消或更改该条款。即使它通过成为法律，交通部在如何以及何时实施该要求方面也有很大的回旋余地，并且很容易将其推迟到国会设定的时间表之外。
尽管如此，汽车安全倡导者仍认为这是一个打击不良驾驶的难得机会，据估计，不良驾驶每年导致多达 10,000 人死亡。 “这需要一些时间，但你必须从某个地方开始，”公路和汽车安全倡导组织总裁凯茜蔡斯说。 “安全带、安全气囊、电子稳定控制：所有这些技术都是从这种规则制定开始的。”尽管如此，新措施可能会面临重大的政治反弹。以前的安全措施本质上是在发生碰撞时保护用户的附加功能。但是，受损驾驶检测器会直接限制车辆的功能，阻止用户在他们选择开车的时候开车。但对于汽车安全倡导者来说，因酒后驾车造成的死亡人数上升实在是太紧迫了，不容忽视。 “我认为 [政治斗争] 是一个非常令人担忧的问题，”蔡斯说，“但我们也有这些受害者的家人被带走，因为有人在受损时开车。”对于像莱文这样的汽车安全倡导者来说，这是一个值得为之奋斗的提议。 “会不会有人不喜欢这个功能？当然，”他说。 “我们所拥有的是一个世代的机会，可以停止在这些纪念馆中添加酒驾受害者名单，而是以一种利用现有技术并追求表演而不是行为的方式来做到这一点。”
note_en:
On Sunday, a bipartisan group of Senators published draft text of  a massive new bipartisan infrastructure bill, proposing more than a trillion dollars in spending and a vast array of far-reaching provisions. But a little-noticed section in the bill could have significant implications in the fight against drunk driving, eventually mandating a new in-car safety technology to actively prevent Americans from driving while impaired.
 Introduced under the heading “Advanced Impaired Driving Technology,” the provision would require the Department of Transportation to set a new standard for detecting and preventing impaired driving. The bill calls on the secretary of transportation to release a standard within three years, with the requirement taking effect for new cars three years after that. The specific provisions of the standard are vague, but it would require cars to “passively monitor the performance of a driver of a motor vehicle to accurately identify whether that driver may be impaired” and “prevent or limit motor vehicle operation” if impairment is detected.
 The system would “passively monitor the performance of a driver ... to identify whether that driver may be impaired”
 The specific means of creating that system are still undetermined, but advocates say much of the technology is already available. Driver monitoring systems, which track a driver’s face or eyelids to ensure they are alert and actively piloting the vehicle, are already offered in some models by  Lexus,  BMW, and  Mercedes Benz. Systems like lane detection could also be used to detect impairment, creating an alert if the driver is consistently veering outside their lane.
 “Twenty years ago, this technology didn’t exist,” says Jason Levine of the Center for Auto Safety. “[But] we have the technology available now. We can install tech in vehicles that helps to monitor whether someone is impaired and stops that person from hurting themselves or others.”
 Crucially, the new standard wouldn’t be limited to drunk drivers. Because the systems measure impairment directly, they would be just as effective at detecting impairment from prescription drugs, emotional distress, or simple distraction. A longer-term effort would also seek to mandate passive alcohol monitoring systems, like those  currently being developed by Volvo.
 While the provisions are aimed at creating a new mandatory requirement for automakers, such a requirement is still a long way off. Negotiations around the infrastructure bill are still in flux, and the provision could still be removed or altered by lawmakers. Even if it passes into law, the Department of Transportation will have wide leeway in how and when to implement the requirement and could easily delay it beyond the schedule set by Congress.
 Still, auto safety advocates see this as a rare opportunity to fight back against impaired driving, which causes as many as 10,000 deaths a year by some estimates. “This will take some time, but you have to start somewhere,” says Cathy Chase, president of Advocates for Highway and Auto Safety. “Seatbelts, airbags, electronic stability control: all of those technologies started with this kind of rulemaking.”
 Still, the new measures could face significant political blowback. Previous safety measures were essentially additional features protecting users in the event of a crash. But an impaired driving detector would directly limit the functionality of the vehicle, preventing users from driving at a moment when they would otherwise choose to drive.
 But for auto safety advocates, the rising death toll from impaired driving is simply too urgent to ignore. “I think [the political fight] is a very real concern,” Chase says, “but we also have these victims whose family members were taken from them because someone got behind the wheel while impaired.”
 For auto safety advocates like Levine, it’s a proposal that’s worth fighting for. “Will there be some people who don’t like this feature? Sure,” he says. “What we’ve got is a generational opportunity to stop adding lists of victims of drunk drivers to these memorials, and do so in a way that is using existing technology and getting after the performance as opposed to the behavior.”
title: 拟议的联邦标准将要求汽车“防止或限制受损驾驶员的操作”
title_en: Proposed Federal Standard Would Require Cars To 'Prevent or Limit Operation' By Impaired Drivers

note: 谷歌已经开始向使用非常老的 Android 设备的用户发送电子邮件，告诉他们是时候说再见了。从 9 月 27 日开始，运行 Android 2.3.7 及更低版本的设备将无法再登录 Google 服务，这实际上扼杀了 Android 上的大部分体验。正如 Google 在官方社区帖子中所说，“如果您在 9 月 27 日之后登录您的设备，当您尝试使用 Gmail、YouTube 和地图等 Google 产品和服务时，您可能会收到用户名或密码错误。” Android 是有史以来基于云的操作系统之一。尤其是在旧版本中，许多包含的应用程序和服务都与您的 Google 登录名相关联，如果这停止工作，您手机的很大一部分就会变砖。虽然 Android 可以在今天不发布完整系统更新的情况下更新许多核心组件，但大约 10 年前发布的 Android 2.3.7 Gingerbread 并不是那么模块化。各个 Google 应用程序开始可通过 Android Market/Play 商店更新，但登录 Google 仍然是系统级服务，并且被及时冻结。任何希望允许从这些版本登录的 Google 服务都必须符合 2011 年时代的安全标准，这意味着关闭双因素身份验证 (2FA) 并在您的 Google 帐户中启用特殊的“允许安全性较低的访问”设置.真的，这些旧的 Android 版本最终不得不消亡，因为它们太不安全了。谷歌在 Android Studio 中显示了 Android 版本的活跃用户群细分，而 Gingerbread 的设备数量如此之少，以至于它甚至没有上榜。它不到活跃设备的 0.2%，落后于 14 个其他版本的 Android。这些旧设备的用户仍然可以侧载第三方应用程序商店并找到所有 Google 应用程序的替代品，但如果您是技术用户并且无法获得新设备，则很有可能您可以加载全新的带有售后市场 Android ROM 的操作系统。 9 月 27 日之后，您可以登录的最旧 Android 版本是 Android 3.0 Honeycomb，该版本仅适用于平板电脑。这个操作系统仍然不是模块化的，但谷歌意识到登录安全更新将在未来成为一个问题，Honeycomb 在初始设置中添加了“通过浏览器登录”选项。硬编码的 Android 登录仍然可以被破坏，但“通过浏览器登录”会将您踢到一个网页——可以用更新的技术更新——然后可以将该登录转发到操作系统。将 Honeycomb 从“不太安全的应用程序”的名称中拯救出来仍然不够，并且不能很好地与 2FA 配合使用，但它足以让操作系统暂时保持运行。登录程序在 Android 5.0 Lollipop 中变得可更新，它会在您登录之前检查初始设置更新。这些设备已经过时了一段时间，因此对于日常使用来说没什么大不了的，但谷歌的服务器关闭是保护主义者的噩梦。今天，任何人都可以启动旧的 Apple II 或在旧计算机上安装 Windows 1.0 并看到完整的体验，但是一旦谷歌切断登录支持，旧版本的 Android 就死了。如果您无法登录 Google，请告别 Android Market、Gmail、Google 地图和 Google Talk。基本操作系统仍然可以工作，但您将无法执行人们在这些手机上实际执行的任何操作。除非使用某种疯狂的登录模拟系统，否则您将永远无法再看到这些应用程序在手机上运行。由于更高的登录安全性，这不是谷歌第一次淘汰旧版本的 Android。 Android 1.0 时代的应用程序已被破坏多年。在 Ars，我们看到了这一切，并在这篇巨文中记录了 Android 的每个早期版本。这些应用程序可能不再工作，但我们将始终拥有屏幕截图。
note_en:
Google has started emailing users of very old Android devices to tell them it's time to say goodbye.
 Starting September 27, devices running Android 2.3.7 and lower will no longer be able to log in to Google services, effectively killing a big portion of the on-rails Android experience. As Google puts it in an official  community post, "If you sign in to your device after September 27, you may get username or password errors when you try to use Google products and services like Gmail, YouTube, and Maps."
 Android is one of the most cloud-based operating systems ever. Especially in older versions, many included apps and services were tied to your Google login, and if that stops working, a large chunk of your phone is bricked. While Android can update many core components without shipping a full system update today, Android 2.3.7 Gingerbread, released around 10 years ago, was not so modular.
 The individual Google apps  started to be updatable through the Android Market/Play Store, but signing in to Google was still a system-level service and is frozen in time. Any Google services wanting to allow sign-ins from those versions would have to conform to 2011-era security standards, which means turning off two-factor authentication (2FA) and enabling a special " allow less-secure access" setting in your Google account. Really, these old Android versions have to die eventually because they're just too insecure. Google shows active user base breakdowns for Android versions in Android Studio, and Gingerbread has such a low device count that it doesn't even make the list. It's less than 0.2 percent of active devices, behind 14 other versions of Android. Users of these old devices could still sideload a third-party app store and find replacements for all the Google apps, but if you're a technical user and can't get a new device, there's a good chance you could load a whole new operating system with an aftermarket Android ROM.
     After September 27, the oldest version of Android you'll be able to sign in to is Android 3.0 Honeycomb, which is only for tablets. This OS still isn't modular, but Google realized that login security updates would start to be an issue in the future, and Honeycomb added a "sign-in via browser" option to the initial setup. The hard-coded Android login can still be broken, but "sign-in via browser" will kick you out to a webpage—which can be updated with newer technology—and can then forward that login to the OS. It's still not enough to save Honeycomb from the "less-secure app" designation and doesn't work well with 2FA, but it's enough to keep the OS trucking for now.
 The login procedure became updatable in  Android 5.0 Lollipop, which checks for initial setup updates  before you even log in.
  These devices have been obsolete for a while, so it's not a big deal for day-to-day usage, but Google's server shutdowns are a nightmare for preservationists. Today, anyone can fire up an old Apple II or install Windows 1.0 on an old computer and see the full experience, but once Google cuts off login support, old versions of Android are  dead. If you can't log in to Google, say goodbye to the Android Market, Gmail, Google Maps, and Google Talk. The base OS will still work, but you won't be able to do anything people actually did on these phones. You'll never  be able to see these apps work on the phones again, barring some kind of crazy login emulation system. This isn't the first time Google has killed off older versions of Android because of higher login security. The Android 1.0-era apps have been broken for years. Here at Ars, we saw all this coming and documented every early version of Android in  this giant article. The apps might not work anymore, but we'll always have the screenshots.
title: 谷歌下个月将淘汰非常旧版本的 Android
title_en: Google Will Kill Off Very Old Versions of Android Next Month

note: 今天，纽约市市长比尔·德布拉西奥 (Bill De Blasio) 宣布了“纽约通行证的关键”的详细信息，该计划要求在进入健身中心、餐厅或娱乐场所等场所之前提供针对 COVID-19 的疫苗接种证明。纽约人可以通过携带疫苗接种卡或将其扫描并存储在两个授权的移动应用程序之一中来满足这些要求。虽然一些企业和活动已经开始要求工人和顾客提供疫苗接种证明或最近的 COVID 测试呈阴性，但政府规定的“疫苗护照”一直是一个有争议的话题。很快，如果没有#COVID19 疫苗的证明，您将无法进入纽约市的餐厅、健身房、音乐会或表演。立即加入我们以获取更多信息。 https://t.co/oVKhBC3Ptr — 纽约市市长办公室 (@NYCMayorsOffice) 2021 年 8 月 3 日 高度传染性 Delta 变体的传播被认为是在不恢复全面封锁或其他措施的情况下增加限制的原因。该计划计划于 8 月 13 日启动，执行计划于 9 月 13 日开始。它没有引入任何新的文档；这个名字是对它作为城市复苏的“钥匙”的引用。工作人员和顾客可以通过以下三种方式之一确认他们的疫苗接种状态（至少接种一剂）：正如路透社所提到的，纽约市已经开始了其他几项让人们接种疫苗的举措，包括强制要求市政府雇员（或者每周接受一次检测）并为公众提供 100 美元的奖励。纽约市的数据显示，大约 60% 的居民至少接种了一剂疫苗。
note_en:
Today, NYC Mayor Bill De Blasio  announced details of the “Key to NYC Pass,” a plan to require proof of vaccination against COVID-19 before entering locations like fitness centers, restaurants, or entertainment venues. New Yorkers can meet those requirements by carrying their vaccination card or scanning and storing it in one of two authorized mobile apps. While some businesses and events have already begun to require proof of vaccination or a recent negative COVID test for entry by workers and customers, government-mandated “ vaccine passports” have been a controversial topic.
 Soon you will not be able to go inside a restaurant, inside a gym, or inside a concert or performance in New York City without proof of a  #COVID19 vaccine. Join us now for more info.  https://t.co/oVKhBC3Ptr
— NYC Mayor's Office (@NYCMayorsOffice)  August 3, 2021
 The spread of the highly contagious Delta variant is being cited as a reason to increase restrictions without returning to a full lockdown or other measures. The program is scheduled to launch on August 13th, with enforcement slated to start on September 13th. It doesn’t introduce any new documentation; the name is a reference to it serving as a “key” to the city’s recovery.
 Workers and patrons can confirm their vaccination status (at least one dose administered) in one of three ways:
  As   Reuters mentions, NYC has already begun several other initiatives to get people vaccinated, including a mandate for city employees (or else face weekly testing) and a $100 incentive for the public. NYC data shows about 60 percent of residents have received at least one dose of a vaccine.

note: AMD 正式揭开了其最新的 APU，Ryzen 7 5700G 和 Ryzen 5 5600G 的面纱。正如他们的品牌所暗示的那样，这些新产品基于与锐龙 5000 系列处理器相同的技术，即 AMD 出色的 Zen 3 核心架构，但也具有集成图形功能，因此被称为“G”。然而，这些特定的处理器标志着与类似的上一代 AMD 产品有所不同。虽然 Ryzen 3000G 系列 APU 被降级为最多 4 个内核且 CPU 和 GPU 时钟速度有些慢的入门级系统，但 Ryzen 5000G APU 通过提供多达 8 个内核，最大 CPU 加速时钟超过 4.4 GHz，更快的内存支持，以及显着更高的 GPU 时钟速度。然而，片上图形仍然基于 AMD 的 Vega GPU 架构。当然，AMD 在技术上确实发布了具有更高时钟和核心数的 Ryzen 和 Ryzen PRO 4000G 系列处理器，但这些都是 OEM 专用处理器，并不打算以零售方式出售。与标准的 Ryzen 5000 系列处理器相比，AMD 通过 Ryzen 7 5700G 和 Ryzen 5 5600G 瞄准更多主流应用。两款芯片均具有 65W TDP 和插槽，可直接插入 AMD 的插槽 AM4 平台。这些处理器基本上适用于任何基于 400 或 500 系列芯片组的主板，前提是制造商已使用必要的 AGESA 更新了主板的 BIOS。 POST/启动这些 APU 需要 AGESA 1.1.8.0，但建议使用 AGESA 1.2.0.3b（或更新版本）以获得最佳性能。锐龙7 5700G显然是这两款CPU的高端机型。它是一个 8 核/16 线程处理器，具有 4MB 的 L2 缓存和 16MB 的 L3。这些 CPU 内核与 8 CU Radeon Vega 图形引擎配合使用，并具有 24 条 PCIe Gen 3 连接通道。 5700G 的基本 CPU 时钟为 3.8GHz，最大升压时钟为 4.6GHz。处理器上的 GPU 可提升至 2GHz，与上一代 3000 系列 APU 的 1.4GHz 相比有了大幅提升。 Ryzen 5 5600G 具有 6 个 CPU 内核（12 个线程）和相应较小的 3MB L2 缓存，将性能降低了一个档次；但是，L3 缓存大小保持不变。 iGPU 也略有缩小，只有 7 个 CU。在 3.9GHz 时，5600G 的基本时钟比 5700G 高 100MHz，但其最大提升频率为 4.4GHz。 iGPU 的最大时钟速度也略低于 1.9GHz。 AMD 将这两款 APU 定位于 Intel Core i7-11700 和 Core i6-11600。 Ryzen 7 5700G 的建议价格为 359 美元，Ryzen 5 5600G 的建议价格为 259 美元，整整少了 100 美元。这两个价格都与英特尔的竞争产品完全一致。然而，从 Ryzen 7 5800X 和 Ryzen 5 5600X 的当前定价来看，这些新的 APU 的定价要低得多，考虑到它们的 CPU 内核数量相似，而且基本上是“免费”的 GPU，这很好。 Ryzen 7 5700G 和 Ryzen 5 5600G 可能是 AMD 的 Infinity Fabric。 Infinity Fabric 可以灵活地链接各种芯片上的 IP，结合 AMD 当前的 CPU 架构，基本上允许 AMD 将 Zen 3 放入设计中，作为上一代 Zen 2 架构的替代品。使用这些芯片，最多 8 核/16 线程 Zen 3 CCD 可连接到最多 8 个计算单元 Radeon Vega iGPU 和相邻的多媒体引擎。这些芯片是在台积电的 7nm 节点上生产的，处理器直接进入 AMD 现有的插槽 AM4 生态系统——同样，假设主板已经更新了必要的 AGESA（可能已经更新）。从外观上看，锐龙 7 5700G 和锐龙 5 5600G 与 AMD 目前所有的主流处理器没什么两样。包装、引脚、集成散热器和品牌都是一样的。只有印在顶部的型号发生了变化。启动最新版本的 CPU-Z 可以一瞥这些新的 Ryzen 5000G 系列处理器的配置。每个内核有 64KB（32KB 数据 + 32KB 指令）的 L1 缓存，每个内核有 512KB 的 L2 缓存，板载每个 CCD 有一个 16MB 的 L3 池。时钟和电压会根据负载而变化（稍后将在超频部分详细介绍），并且 5700G 和 5600G 之间的系列、型号、步进和版本是相同的。抛开所有这些，让我们看看这些最新的 APU 表现如何，好吗？
note_en: AMD is officially taking the wraps off of its latest APUs, the  Ryzen 7 5700G and the Ryzen 5 5600G. As their branding suggests, these new products are based on the same technology as Ryzen 5000 series processors, namely AMD’s excellent Zen 3 core architecture, but with integrated graphics capabilities as well, hence the “G” designation.   These particular processors, however, mark somewhat of a departure from similar, previous-gen AMD products. While Ryzen 3000G series APUs were relegated to entry-level systems with a maximum of 4 cores and somewhat tame CPU and GPU clock speeds, Ryzen 5000G APUs crank things up a few notches by offering up to 8 cores, with max CPU boost clocks exceeding 4.4GHz, faster memory support, and significantly higher GPU clock speeds as well. The on-die graphics, however, are still based on AMD’s  Vega GPU architecture. Of course, AMD did technically release Ryzen and Ryzen PRO 4000G series processors with higher clocks and core counts too, but those were OEM-only processors and weren't meant to be sold at retail.   AMD is targeting more mainstream applications with the Ryzen 7 5700G and the Ryzen 5 5600G versus standard Ryzen 5000 series processors. Both chips feature a 65W TDP and slot right into AMD’s socket  AM4 platform. These processors will essentially work with any 400 or 500 series chipset-based motherboards, provided the manufacture has updated the board's BIOS with the necessary AGESA. AGESA 1.1.8.0 is required to POST/boot these APUs, but AGESA 1.2.0.3b (or newer) is recommended for optimal performance.    The Ryzen 7 5700G is obviously the higher-end model of these two CPUs. It is an 8-core / 16-thread processor with 4MB of L2 cache and 16MB of L3. Those CPU cores are mated to an 8 CU Radeon Vega graphics engine, and it has 24 lanes of PCIe Gen 3 connectivity. The 5700G’s base CPU clock is 3.8GHz, with a maximum boost clock of 4.6GHz. The on-processor GPU can boost up to 2GHz, which is a massive uptick from the 1.4GHz of previous-gen 3000-series APUs. The Ryzen 5 5600G takes things down a notch with 6 CPU cores (12 threads), and a commensurately smaller 3MB of L2 cache; the L3 cache size remains unchanged, though. The iGPU is scaled down slightly as well, and has only 7 CUs. At 3.9GHz, the 5600G’s base clock is 100MHz higher than the 5700G’s, but its max boost lands at 4.4GHz. The iGPU has a slightly lower 1.9GHz maximum clock speed too.   AMD is positioning these two APUs against the Intel Core i7-11700 and Core i6-11600. The Ryzen 7 5700G’s suggested price is $359 and the Ryzen 5 5600G is a full $100 less at $259. Both of those prices are exactly in-line with Intel’s competitive offerings. Looking at current pricing for the  Ryzen 7 5800X and Ryzen 5 5600X, however, these new APUs arrive at significantly lower pricing, which is great considering their similar CPU core counts with essentially a “free” GPU thrown in.   The key ingredient that made the Ryzen 7 5700G and the Ryzen 5 5600G possible is AMD’s Infinity Fabric. The flexibility of the Infinity Fabric to link various IP on-die, in conjunction with AMD’s current CPU architecture, essentially allowed AMD to drop  Zen 3 into the design as a replacement to the previous-gen Zen 2 architecture. With these chips, an up-to 8-core / 16-thread Zen 3 CCD is linked to an up-to 8 Compute Unit Radeon Vega iGPU and adjacent multi-media engines. The chips are produced on TSMC’s 7nm node, and the processors drop right into AMD’s existing socket AM4 ecosystem – again, assuming the motherboard has been updated with the necessary AGESA (which is probably has been).      From the outside the Ryzen 7 5700G and Ryzen 5 5600G look just like all of AMD’s current mainstream processors. The packaging, pin-out, integrated heat-spreaders and branding are all the same. It’s only the model number emblazoned on top that changes.     Firing up the latest edition of CPU-Z gives a glimpse into the configuration of these new Ryzen 5000G-series processors. There’s 64KB (32KB data + 32KB instructions) of L1 cache per core, 512KB of L2 cache per core, and a single pool of 16MB of L3 per CCD on board. Clocks and voltages will vary based on load (more on this later in the overclocking section), and the family, model, stepping and revision are identical between the 5700G and 5600G.  With all of that out of the way, let’s see how these latest APUs perform, shall we?
title: AMD 锐龙 5000G 系列以超值价格推出集成显卡
title_en: AMD Ryzen 5000G Series Launches With Integrated Graphics At Value Price Points

note:
1967 年 7 月的一个早晨，两架美国 B-52 轰炸机在接近当时位于南越的目标时在南海上空相撞。七名机组人员逃脱，但来自空军、海军和海岸警卫队的救援部队无法找到其他六名男子，其中包括一名来自纽约的导航员保罗·A·阿沃莱斯少校。直到去年，科学家们才扫描海底发现了其中一架 B-52，并找到了 Avolese 少校的遗骸。 “在一个被证明是圣地的地方潜水，并意识到也许我们有能力帮助那些失去这位失踪飞行员的家庭重新关闭，这让我感到非常谦卑，”埃里克·J·特里尔 (Eric J. Terrill) 说。两名潜入沉船的潜水员。科学家们说，这次复苏突显出五角大楼搜寻越南战争中失踪人员的能力发生了转变。几十年来，这些努力主要集中在前冲突地区的土地上。但在这种情况下，美国调查人员使用高科技机器人查看了越南漫长海岸线附近的一个水下地点。他们对该技术的使用是更大趋势的一部分。为美国国家海洋和大气管理局管理 9 架飞机和 16 艘研究和调查船的海军少将 Nancy Hann 说，机器人水下和水面航行器“正迅速成为海洋科学和探索不可或缺的工具”。汉恩海军上将说：“在绘制海底地图、定位和测量沉船和其他沉没物体以及在船舶和其他车辆不易到达的地方收集数据时，它们已被证明是一种力量倍增器。”
非营利组织 Project Recover 的首席考古学家安德鲁·皮特鲁斯卡 (Andrew Pietruszka) 表示，重新关注越南海底坠毁地点的一个原因是，许多陆上线索已经耗尽。该小组与国防部战俘/MIA 会计机构 (DPAA) 一起执行最近的恢复任务，该机构是五角大楼的一个部门，负责寻找和返回阵亡的军事人员。 “随着时间的推移，他们已经完成了很多非常好的土地案例和地点，他们已经处理了它们，”前 DPAA 法医考古学家 Pietruszka 先生说，他现在在斯克里普斯海洋学研究所工作。加州大学圣地亚哥分校。 “现在，大多数未被观察过的地点都落入了那个水下领域，”他补充道。 DPAA 的最新数据显示，截至 7 月下旬，越南战争中仍有 1,584 名美国人员失踪。该机构专门研究战争的分析师鲍勃·马夫斯 (Bob Maves) 表示，据信其中 420 艘在越南海岸线或领海内失踪。他说，从技术上讲，该机构尚未在越南调查过的大多数行动中失踪地点都在水下，这是“技术上正确的”，但补充说，大多数都位于太深的地方，无法进行恢复行动。寻找 Avolese 少校的 B-52 轰炸机的任务是国防机构第一次允许非政府合作伙伴在越南开展工作。 “我们希望，而且双方已经讨论过这个问题，希望这是我们可以共同开展的许多项目中的第一个，当然，与越南政府合作，”皮特鲁什卡先生说。 Avolese少校的遗体在一个有可能成为中美之间日益激烈对抗的导火索的地区被发现。北京一直在南海建造人工岛屿并将其军事化。这让美国以及越南和其他在该地区拥有海洋主权的国家感到担忧。
允许美国人将先进的水下机器人带入越南控制的南中国海地区——这些设备可能对两国政府都有军事用途——在外交上也很敏感。 Pietruszka 先生说，获得最近探险的许可“对各方来说都是一项艰巨的任务”。但自 1995 年两国关系正常化以来，美越关系稳步升温。对越南来说，允许此类项目是与前敌人建立进一步信任的一种方式，退休的越南少将 Le Van Cuong 说。 “越南人的突出特点是乐于助人，”他补充说。档案军事文件显示，保罗·安德鲁·阿沃莱斯 (Paul Andrew Avolese) 出生于 1932 年 6 月 12 日，其家人拒绝接受采访。他来自纽约，曾在越南空军的 4133d 轰炸机联队服役。文件显示，1967 年 7 月 7 日，他和他的机组人员与其他 B-52 从关岛的一个美国基地起飞，轰炸南越的一个目标。当两架轰炸机在当时的南越首都西贡东南约 65 英里处机动到位时，它们相撞，点燃了“火球”。 Avolese少校飞机上的一个人，威廉·J·克鲁姆少将，是战争中阵亡的几位美国将军中的第一位。坠机事件发生八天后，空军的 Mitchell A. Cobeaga 上校在一封信中告诉 Avolese 少校的父母，坠机的确切原因尚不清楚。 “4133d Bomb Wing 的每个人都和你的儿子一样担心，”他补充道。事故发生时 35 岁的 Avolese 少校在写信几天后被宣布死亡。美国军方后来将他的遗体以及其他五名失踪者的遗体归类为“不可恢复”。尽管如此，几十年来，调查人员一直在寻找有关两架 B-52 残骸的潜在线索。
去年 2 月，Project Recover 的科学家帮助国防部战俘/MIA 会计局继续寻找以前失败的 B-52。他们的调查船为期两周的远征目标是一个约八平方英里的海底区域。 “我想说，就我们应该在哪里搜索而言，大海捞针非常大，”Project Recover 的首席研究员 Terrill 博士说。他说，在过去，此类探险将依赖潜水员或“相当初级”的声纳。但是这个使用了三个 6 英尺长的机器人，能够在两侧扫描 229 英尺，并生成海底的高分辨率图像。一架 B-52 的残骸映入眼帘。然后该团队在海床上看到了人类遗骸。但特里尔博士说，在回收它们之前，船员需要通过卫星电话获得越南和美国军方官员的许可。大约 24 小时后，遗体被带到了地表。夏威夷的一个实验室最终确定它们是 Avolese 少校的。还没有找到另外两名乘坐 B-52 的人的遗骸。 Pietruszka 先生说，在这种情况下，下一步通常会进行全面挖掘以恢复它们，而 DPAA 将做出这个决定。 9 月，五角大楼正式对 Avolese 少校负责。它说，在檀香山太平洋国家纪念公墓的失踪者法庭，将在他的名字旁边放置一个仪式别针，以表明他已被找到。
note_en:
On a July morning in 1967, two American B-52 bombers collided over the South China Sea as they approached a target in what was then South Vietnam.
 Seven crew members escaped, but rescue units from the Air Force, Navy and Coast Guard were unable to find six other men, including a navigator from New York, Maj. Paul A. Avolese. It wasn’t until last year that scientists scanning the seafloor found one of the B-52s and recovered Major Avolese’s remains.
 “It was very humbling to be diving a site that turned out as hallowed ground, and realizing that maybe we were in a position to help bring closure back to families that had been missing this lost aviator,” said Eric J. Terrill, one of two divers who descended to the wreck.
 Scientists say the recovery highlights a shift in the  Pentagon’s ability to search for personnel still missing from the Vietnam War.
 For decades, such efforts have mainly focused on land in former conflict zones. But in this case, American investigators looked at an underwater site near Vietnam’s long coastline, using high-tech robots.
 Their use of that technology is part of a larger trend. Robotic underwater and surface vehicles are “rapidly becoming indispensable tools for ocean science and exploration,” said Rear Adm. Nancy Hann, who manages a fleet of nine aircraft and 16 research and survey vessels for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.
 “They have proven to be a force multiplier when it comes to mapping the seafloor, locating and surveying wrecks and other sunken objects, and collecting data in places not easily accessed by ships and other vehicles,” Admiral Hann said.
  One reason for the new focus on Vietnam’s undersea crash sites is that many land-based leads have been exhausted, said Andrew Pietruszka, the lead archaeologist for Project Recover, a nonprofit organization. The group worked on the recent recovery mission with the Defense POW/MIA Accounting Agency, or D.P.A.A., the arm of the Pentagon tasked with finding and returning fallen military personnel.
 “Over time, a lot of the really good land cases and sites they’ve already done, they’ve already processed them,” said Mr. Pietruszka, a former forensic archaeologist for D.P.A.A. who now works for the Scripps Institution of Oceanography at the University of California, San Diego. “Now the majority of sites that haven’t been looked at are falling in that underwater realm,” he added.
 As of late July, 1,584 U.S. personnel were still missing from the Vietnam War, the D.P.A.A.’s  latest figures show. Bob Maves, an analyst for the agency who specializes in the war, said 420 of them were believed to have been lost along Vietnam’s coastline or within its territorial waters.
 He said it was “technically true” that the majority of missing-in-action sites the agency has not yet looked at in Vietnam were underwater, but added that most were in places too deep for recovery operations.
 The mission to find Major Avolese’s B-52 bomber was the first time that the defense agency had allowed a nongovernmental partner to conduct work in Vietnam.
 “Our hope, and both sides have discussed this, is that this is the first of hopefully many projects that we can be doing together, in conjunction, of course, with the Vietnamese government,” Mr. Pietruszka said.
  Major Avolese’s remains were recovered in a region that threatens to become a flash point in the  intensifying confrontation between China and the United States. Beijing has been building artificial islands in  the South China Sea and militarizing them. That has worried the United States, as well as Vietnam and other countries that have maritime claims in the region.
 Allowing Americans to bring advanced underwater robots into Vietnamese-controlled areas of the South China Sea — equipment that could have military applications for both governments — is also diplomatically sensitive. Mr. Pietruszka said obtaining the permissions for the recent expedition was “a heavy lift for all parties.”
 But U.S.-Vietnamese ties have steadily warmed since the two countries  normalized relations in 1995. And for Vietnam, allowing such projects is one way of building further trust with its former enemy, said Le Van Cuong, a retired Vietnamese major general.
 “The outstanding characteristic of Vietnamese people is the desire to help others,” he added.
  Paul Andrew Avolese, whose family declined to be interviewed, was born on June 12, 1932, archival military documents show. He was from New York and served in the Air Force’s 4133d Bomb Wing in Vietnam.
 On July 7, 1967, he and his crew were flying from a U.S. base in Guam alongside other B-52s to bomb a target in South Vietnam, documents show. As two of the bombers maneuvered into place about 65 miles southeast of what was then Saigon, the South Vietnamese capital, they collided, igniting a “fireball.” One person on Major Avolese’s plane, Maj. Gen. William J. Crumm, was the first of  several American generals killed in the war.
  Eight days after the crash, Col. Mitchell A. Cobeaga of the Air Force told Major Avolese’s parents in a letter that the exact cause of the collision was unknown. “Every man here in the 4133d Bomb Wing shares your anxiety over your son,” he added.
 Major Avolese, who was 35 at the time of the crash, was declared dead a few days after the letter was written. The U.S. military later classified his remains, as well as those of the five others missing, as “nonrecoverable.” Still, investigators pursued potential leads about the wreckage of the two B-52s for decades.
   In February of last year, scientists from Project Recover helped the Defense POW/MIA Accounting Agency continue the search for the B-52s where previous ones had failed. The target of their survey vessel’s two-week expedition was a seafloor area measuring about eight square miles.
 “The haystack was very large, I would say, in terms of where we should search,” said Dr. Terrill, a principal investigator at Project Recover.
 In the past, such expeditions would have relied on divers or “fairly rudimentary” sonar, he said. But this one used three six-foot-long robots with the ability to scan up to 229 feet on either side and to produce high-resolution imagery of the seafloor.
 The wreckage of one B-52 came into view. Then the team saw human remains on the seabed. But before recovering them, Dr. Terrill said, the crew needed to obtain permission from military officials in Vietnam and the United States by satellite phone.
 About 24 hours later, the remains were brought to the surface. A lab in Hawaii eventually identified them as Major Avolese’s.
 The remains of two other men who were on the same B-52 have yet to be found. Mr. Pietruszka said a full excavation to recover them would typically be the next step in such a case, and that D.P.A.A. would make that decision.
 In September, Major Avolese was officially accounted for by the Pentagon. It said a ceremonial pin would be placed next to his name at the Courts of the Missing, in the National Memorial Cemetery of the Pacific in Honolulu, to indicate that he had been found.
title: 海底机器人找到了自 1967 年以来失踪的空军领航员
title_en: With Undersea Robots, an Air Force Navigator Lost Since 1967 Is Found

知识产权律师说决定很糟糕，因为我们最不需要的就是机器人专利巨魔澳大利亚法院已经决定，人工智能可以在专利申请中被认定为发明人。在斯蒂芬泰勒提起的一起案件中，澳大利亚联邦法院上个月在其他司法管辖区提起并败诉了类似案件，并裁定该国专利专员在决定人工智能不能被视为发明者时犯了错误。比奇法官得出了这个结论，因为澳大利亚法律没有规定专利申请人必须是人类。正如比奇的判断所说：“……在我看来，就该法案而言，人工智能系统可以是发明者。”首先，发明者是代理人名词；代理人可以是发明创造的人或事物。其次，如此持有反映了许多其他可申请专利的发明的现实，在这些发明中，不能明智地说人类是发明者。第三，该法案没有规定相反的结论。”法官还担心专利专员拒绝 Thaler 专利提交的逻辑是错误的。
“根据专员的逻辑，如果你有一项可申请专利的发明但没有人类发明者，你就不能申请专利，”判决指出。 “法案中没有任何内容可以证明这种结果是合理的。”因此，Beach 法官将 Thaler 的申请发回给专利专员，并指示重新考虑拒绝的原因。 Thaler 以 DABUS 的名义在世界各地提交了专利申请——一种用于统一感知的自主引导的设备。 DABUS 发明的产品包括食品容器和发光信标。英国、美国和欧盟没有选择采用类似的逻辑——事实上，美国规定发明者必须是人类。然而，DABUS 上周在南非获得了一项食品容器专利。澳大利亚知识产权律师马克萨默菲尔德强烈批评比奇法官的决定，理由是它可能会产生垃圾专利。 “仅仅因为专利是（或者至少可以是）好的，并不意味着更多的专利，以更多的方式，由更多的实体产生，就一定更好，”他在他的专利学博客上写道。 “我不认为这个决定......为澳大利亚的利益服务，”萨默菲尔德补充道。 “我认为它代表了一种导致政策制定的司法激进主义形式——在这种情况下，谁或什么的重要问题可以构成授予可对广大公众强制执行的专利垄断的基础——从板凳上。”
在接受《卫报》澳大利亚版采访时，萨默菲尔德提出了机器产生的发明将获得大量专利的前景，创造了如此多的专利，以至于其他创新变得不可能。在他的博客中，萨默菲尔德建议“排除那些不是由人类发明家设计的发明可能是完全合理的。”这完全取决于您希望通过专利制度激励什么类型的活动。 “如果你想促进人类创造力的进步，同时为自动化创新提供较少（或没有）奖励，那么拒绝机器制造的发明专利是完全合理的政策。”萨默菲尔德写道，他希望澳大利亚的专利当局上诉并获胜，因为比奇法官的决定“既不像法官认为的那样聪明，也不像法官认为的那样进步”。 ® 无纸化办公？ 2020 年印刷 2.8 万亿页，下降 14% 或 4500 亿张大品牌可能会感到欣慰的是，到 2025 年，每分钟仍将印刷 440 万页
由于 COVID-19 扰乱了工作世界，2020 年家庭和办公设备打印的页面减少了约 4500 亿页。近来的趋势很明显：即使在大流行爆发之前，人们的打印量就减少了，但去年的下降幅度更大，因为根据 IDC 的数据，打印量比 2019 年的水平下降了 14%，达到 2.8 万亿页。继续阅读 8 年前，另一位亿万富翁将数百万人投入太空以获取太阳能并将其传回地球亿万富翁唐纳德·布伦 (Donald Bren) 在 2013 年默默捐款 1 亿美元，该捐款建立了加州理工学院的天基太阳能项目 (SSPP)，试图加州私立研究大学本周透露，利用来自外太空的太阳能。这位房地产大亨的灵感来自 2011 年《大众科学》（Popular Science）上的一篇文章（也许是这篇？）。他还从他的总体规划城市（如加利福尼亚州尔湾）的总体规划经验中了解了一两件有关配电问题的知识。布伦随后联系加州理工学院讨论他的想法。加州理工学院表示，他在这项技术中没有任何股份，也不会从中获利。由于 SSPP 希望突出即将到来的项目里程碑，现在正在披露这笔捐款，也就是 8 年后。

IP lawyer says decision is bad because the last thing we need is robot patent trolls
  An Australian Court has decided that an artificial intelligence can be recognised as an inventor in a patent submission.
 In a case brought by Stephen Thaler, who has  filed and lost similar cases in other jurisdictions, Australia's Federal Court last month heard and decided that the nation's Commissioner of Patents erred when deciding that an AI can't be considered an inventor.
 Justice Beach reached that conclusion because nothing in Australia law says the applicant for a patent must be human.
   As Beach's judgement  puts it: "… in my view an artificial intelligence system can be an inventor for the purposes of the Act.
     "First, an inventor is an agent noun; an agent can be a person or thing that invents. Second, so to hold reflects the reality in terms of many otherwise patentable inventions where it cannot sensibly be said that a human is the inventor. Third, nothing in the Act dictates the contrary conclusion."
 The Justice also worried that the Commissioner of Patents' logic in rejecting Thaler's patent submissions was faulty.
   "On the Commissioner's logic, if you had a patentable invention but no human inventor, you could not apply for a patent," the judgement states. "Nothing in the Act justifies such a result."
 Justice Beach therefore sent Thaler's applications back to the Commissioner of Patents, with instructions to re-consider the reasons for their rejection.
 Thaler has filed patent applications around the world in the name of DABUS – a Device for the Autonomous Boot-strapping of Unified Sentience. Among the items DABUS has invented are a food container and a light-emitting beacon.
   The UK, USA and European Union have not chosen to adopt similar logic – and indeed the USA specifies that inventors must be human. However DABUS was granted a patent in South Africa last week, for its food container.
  Australian intellectual property lawyer Mark Summerfield has strongly criticised Justice Beach's decision on grounds that it could produce junk patents.
 "Just because patents are (or, at least, can be) good, it does not follow that more patents, generated in more ways, by more entities, must be better," he wrote on his  Patentology blog.
 "I do not consider the decision … to serve Australia’s interests," Summerfield added. "I think that it represents a form of judicial activism that results in the development of policy – in this case, the important matter of who, or what, can form the basis for the grant of a patent monopoly enforceable against the public at large – from the bench."
 Speaking to  Guardian Australia, Summerfield  raised the prospect of a flood of patents being awarded to machine-generated inventions, creating so many patents that other innovation becomes impossible.
 On his blog, Summerfield suggested "it may be perfectly reasonable to exclude those inventions that have not been devised by a human inventor.
 "It all depends upon what types of activity you wish to incentivise through the patent system.
 "If you want to promote the advancement of human ingenuity, while offering lesser (or no) rewards for automated innovation, then it is perfectly sound policy to deny patents on machine-made inventions."
 Summerfield wrote that he hopes Australia's patent authorities appeal – and win – because Justice Beach's decision is "neither as clever nor as progressive as the judge appears to believe." ®
     Paperless office? 2.8 trillion pages printed in 2020, down by 14% or 450 billion sheets
  Big brands might take solace that in 2025, 4.4 million pages will still be printed every minute
     Around 450 billion fewer pages were printed from home and office devices in 2020 as COVID-19 disrupted the world of work.
  The direction of travel has been obvious in recent times: people were printing less even before the pandemic took hold, but the decline was sharper last year as volumes plunged 14 per cent on 2019 levels to a total of 2.8 trillion pages, according to IDC.

  8 years ago another billionaire ploughed millions into space to harvest solar power and beam it back down to Earth
     Billionaire Donald Bren was behind a quiet $100m donation in 2013 that established Caltech's Space-based Solar Power Project (SSPP) in an attempt to harness solar power from outer space, the California private research university revealed this week.
  The real estate magnate was inspired by a 2011 article in Popular Science ( perhaps this one?). He also knew a thing or two concerning power distribution problems from his experience master planning cities like Irvine, California.
  Bren subsequently approached Caltech to discuss his ideas. Caltech  said he has no stake in the tech and won't make any money from it. The donation is being disclosed now, eight years later, as SSPP wants to highlight upcoming project milestones.
  Continue reading
  Das tut mir leid! Germany's ruling party sorry for calling cops on researcher after she outed canvassing app flaws
     A "left-wing" German infosec researcher was this week threatened with criminal prosecution after revealing that an app used by Angela Merkel's political party to canvass voters was secretly collecting personal data.
  Germany's respected Chaos Computer Club (CCC)  announced it would stop reporting any weaknesses in the centre-right wing Christian Democratic Union's (CDU) web-facing infrastructure to the party after it procured a criminal prosecution against Lilith Wittmann.
  "I got an email from the Cyber Security Police of Berlin," she told  The Register. "Could you please provide us your address, so we can send you... legal documents? And then I was like, that's weird. I didn't do anything wrong. Let's tweet about that. Let's find a lawyer who can look into that."
  Continue reading
  The Chia cryptocurrency craze is fuelling record sales growth in Europe among distributors of hard disk drives (HDD), according to calendar Q2 shipment data from venerable number cruncher Context.
  Stats for the three months show sales to end users, via distributors and resellers, went up by 141 per cent year-on-year to 454,512 – the biggest quarter in the regional channel ever, said Context.
  Nearline HDDs – the highest capacity drives – led the pack. Sales of the 18TB model swelled to 84,726, up 125 per cent from the prior quarter. 16TB drives, the second most favoured spec, grew to 51,515 units. Other HDD categories also continue to be highly prized thanks to Chia.
  Continue reading
  Please, no Moore: 'Law' that defined how chips have been made for decades has run itself into a cul-de-sac
     Feature In 1965, Gordon Moore published a short informal paper, Cramming more components onto integrated circuits.
  In it, he noted [ PDF] that in three years, the optimal cost per component on a chip had dropped by a factor of 10, while the optimal number had increased by the same factor, from 10 to 100. Based on not much more but these few data points and his knowledge of silicon chip development – he was head of R&D at Fairchild Semiconductors, the company that was to seed Silicon Valley – he said that for the next decade, component counts by area could double every year. By 1975, as far as he would look, up to 65,000 components such as transistors could fit on a single chip costing no more than the 100-component chips at the time of publishing.
  He was right. Furthermore, as transistors shrank they used less power and worked faster, leading to stupendous sustained cost/performance improvements. In 1975, eight years after leaving Fairchild to co-found Intel, Moore revised his "law", actually just an observation, to a doubling every two years. But the other predictions in his original paper of revolutions in computing, communication and general electronics had taken hold. The chip industry had the perfect metric to aim for a rolling, virtuous milestone like no other.
  Continue reading
  'This is peak Chrome; a reasonably good idea hampered because it was pushed out thoughtlessly'
     Google has temporarily reversed Chrome's removal of browser alert windows and other prompts created via cross-origin iframes after a rocky rollout over the past two weeks broke web apps and alarmed developers.
  An iframe, or Inline Frame, is a portion of a web page embedded in another web page. When it includes resources from a different origin or domain, it's a cross-origin iframe.
  Since March, 2020, the team behind Chromium, the open-source engine of Chrome, has been planning to limit the capabilities of cross-origin iframes because they're a security liability. Specifically, they allow an embedded resource like an ad to present a prompt as if it were the host domain.
  Continue reading
  Sponsored The pandemic has been an arduous time for businesses, but many have learned some important lessons about remote access security along the way.
  That’s the gist of the recent  2021 Trends in Securing Digital Identities survey of 500 US security professionals on behalf of the Identity Defined Security Alliance (IDSA), which uncovered a new interest in the fraught topic of identity and access management (IAM).
  It’s hard to think of anything more likely to overwhelm an organisation’s remote access systems than an overnight, life-threatening pandemic, starting with large numbers of employees stuck at home, a shortage of suitably secure laptops, and the problem of inadequate VPN capacity. All of this has been well covered. But even when organisations got on top of that chore list, other, harder-to-solve problems quickly raised their heads.
  Continue reading
  'Some forms of encryption used today can be broken by future large-scale quantum computers, which drives a search for alternatives'
     Amazon Web Services has partnered with the National University of Singapore (NUS) in hope of improving quantum technologies and their applications. The duo announced they had signed a Memorandum of Understanding this week.
  The collaboration will be led by the NUS-hosted Quantum Engineering Program ( QEP), a five-year SG$25m ($18.5m, £13.3m, €15.6m) initiative launched in 2018 by Singapore's National Research Foundation that focuses on translating the often abstract science of quantum physics into tech that can be commercialized.
  So far, the QEP has supported eight major research projects – such as hardware and software that may be able to eventually outperform today’s supercomputers and simulate chemicals to help design drugs – though these solutions remain unfinished, waiting until future-gen quantum computers can make them a reality.
  Continue reading
  Remember Google Plus? Remember its privacy blunder? Remember applying for a slice of a settlement?
  Congrats, $2.15 is on its way to you. And millions on lawyers and admin costs
     Check your bank accounts this month. A settlement payment from Google, regarding a privacy hole in its now-defunct Google+ social network, may be winging its way to you. All $2.15 of it.
  The  vulnerability in question was in the Google+ People API. It could have been exploited by third-party apps to swipe people’s personal information, such as their names, email addresses, genders, and ages, even if this data wasn't publicly visible.
  At first it was thought 438 apps could have siphoned off the records from up to 500,000 users. It was  later discovered that actually a whopping 52.5 million accounts were at risk. It's believed no data was actually stolen by apps.
  Continue reading
  Amazon interfered with a formal election by its warehouse workers in Alabama to unionize – and staff ought to be given a second chance to vote again, an official at the US National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) has concluded.
  After pro-union employees, represented by the Retail, Wholesale and Department Store Union (RWDSU), working at the BHM1 fulfillment center in Bessemer in the Cotton State  lost their unionization election in April, the union swiftly filed objections to the labor relations board.
  "A free and fair election was impossible," said [ PDF] Kerstin Meyers, an NLRB hearing officer, this week. "Under the circumstances, I recommend that a second election be ordered."
  Continue reading
  Facebook, which has repeatedly touted its transparency efforts, on Tuesday disabled the accounts of independent ad transparency researchers.
  The targeted ad biz said it did so in the name of privacy, a source of  persistent scandal for the corporation. Facebook said it disabled the accounts, apps, Pages, and platform access for NYU’s Ad Observatory Project and participating researchers because their work violated its rules.
  "NYU’s Ad Observatory project studied political ads using unauthorized means to access and collect data from Facebook, in violation of our Terms of Service," said Mike Clark, product management director at Facebook, in a  blog post.

The  US military's AI experiments are growing particularly ambitious.  The Drive  reports that US Northern Command recently  completed a string of tests for Global Information Dominance Experiments (GIDE), a combination of AI, cloud computing and sensors that could give the Pentagon the ability to predict events "days in advance," according to Command leader General Glen VanHerck. It's not as mystical as it sounds, but it could lead to a major change in military and government operations.
 The machine learning-based system observes changes in raw, real-time data that hint at possible trouble. If  satellite imagery shows signs that a rival nation's submarine is preparing to leave port, for instance, the AI could flag that mobilization knowing the vessel will likely leave soon. Military analysts can take hours or even days to comb through this information — GIDE technology could send an alert within "seconds," VanHerck said.
  The most recent dry run, GIDE 3, was the most expansive yet. It saw all 11 US commands and the broader Defense Department use a mix of military and civilian sensors to address scenarios where "contested logistics" (such as communications in the Panama Canal) might pose a problem. The technology involved wasn't strictly new, the General said, but the military "stitched everything together."
 The platform could be put into real-world use relatively soon. VanHerck believed the military was "ready to field" the software, and could validate it at the next Globally Integrated Exercise in spring 2022.
 The advantages of this predictive AI are fairly clear. Instead of merely reacting to events or relying on outdated info, the Pentagon could take proactive steps like deploying forces or ramping up defenses. It could also provide an "opportunity" for the civilian government, VanHerck added. He didn't provide examples, but this could help politicians call out acts of aggression while they're still in the early stages.
 There are still limitations. The AI is looking for out-of-the-ordinary clues, such as a greater number of parked cars or aircraft. It can't say for certain what's happening — humans will still be heavily involved. Even so, the tech could be worthwhile if it prevents a 'surprise' attack or leads to negotiations instead of conflict.

note: 亚马逊迄今为止最受期待的原创作品之一，尚未命名的《指环王》原创系列，将于 2022 年 9 月 2 日星期五在 Prime Video 上正式亮相。随着首映日期的确定，亚马逊工作室发布了即将上映的官方第一张图片系列，以中土世界的第二纪元为背景。该系列将发生在 JRR Tolkien 的《霍比特人》和《指环王》两本书中记载的事件之前数千年，它将跟随“熟悉的和新的角色，因为他们面临着长期担心的邪恶再次出现到中东-地球。”在新西兰拍摄后，图像发布与该系列的制作包装有关。粉丝们很快推测该系列将以维林诺为背景，因为图像描绘了似乎是两棵树的东西。这个未命名的项目是亚马逊对其 Prime Video 流媒体服务的巨额投资。据报道，该系列的第一季制作成本约为 4.65 亿美元。对于亚马逊工作室来说，这个系列是一项巨大的创意工作，据报道，《权力的游戏》最后一季每集的成本高达 1500 万美元（尽管其预算最初约为每集 500 万美元）。该系列的主要演员包括辛西娅·阿戴-罗宾逊、罗伯特·阿拉马约、欧文·亚瑟、马克西姆·鲍德里、纳赞宁·博尼亚迪、莫菲德·克拉克、伊斯梅尔·克鲁兹·科尔多瓦、查尔斯·爱德华兹、特里斯坦·格拉维尔、莱尼·亨利爵士、埃玛·霍瓦特、马克拉·卡维纳、约瑟夫·马勒、 Tyroe Muhafidin、Sophia Nomvete、Lloyd Owen、Megan Richards、Dylan Smith、Charlie Vickers、Leon Wadham、Benjamin Walker、Daniel Weyman 和 Sara Zwangobani。 Showrunners JD Payne 和 Patrick McKay 是该节目的执行制作人。 “正如比尔博所说，'现在我想我已经准备好开始另一段旅程了，'”佩恩和麦凯在一份联合声明中说。 “在中土生活和呼吸这几个月是一生的冒险。我们迫不及待地希望粉丝们也有机会这样做。”该系列将于明年在 Prime Video 上播出时在 240 多个地区和国家首映。
note_en:
One of Amazon’s most anticipated originals to date, a yet-unnamed  Lord of the Rings original series, will officially debut on Prime Video on Friday, September 2nd, 2022.
 Along with a premiere date, Amazon Studios released an official first image from the forthcoming series, which will be set in Middle-earth’s Second Age. The series will take place thousands of years before the events chronicled in J. R. R. Tolkien’s  The Hobbit and  The Lord of the Rings books, and it will follow characters “both familiar and new, as they confront the long-feared re-emergence of evil to Middle-earth.”
 The image release is tied to the series’s production wrap after filming in New Zealand. Fans  quickly speculated that the series will be set in Valinor, as the image depicts what appear to be the Two Trees.
 The untitled project is a huge investment by Amazon in its Prime Video streaming service. The series’s first season alone reportedly  cost around $465 million to produce. For context about what a massive creative undertaking this series has been for Amazon Studios, the final season of  Game of Thrones was reported to have cost as much as  $15 million per episode (though its budget was originally around $5 million per episode).
 The headlining cast of the series includes Cynthia Addai-Robinson, Robert Aramayo, Owain Arthur, Maxim Baldry, Nazanin Boniadi, Morfydd Clark, Ismael Cruz Córdova, Charles Edwards, Trystan Gravelle, Sir Lenny Henry, Ema Horvath, Markella Kavenagh, Joseph Mawle, Tyroe Muhafidin, Sophia Nomvete, Lloyd Owen, Megan Richards, Dylan Smith, Charlie Vickers, Leon Wadham, Benjamin Walker, Daniel Weyman, and Sara Zwangobani. Showrunners J.D. Payne and Patrick McKay are executive producing the show.
 “As Bilbo says, ‘Now I think I am quite ready to go on another journey,’” Payne and McKay said in a joint statement. “Living and breathing Middle-earth these many months has been the adventure of a lifetime. We cannot wait for fans to have the chance to do so as well.”
 The series will premiere in more than 240 territories and countries when it arrives on Prime Video next year.
title: 亚马逊的指环王系列将于 2022 年 9 月首播
title_en: Amazon's Lord of the Rings Series Will Premiere In September 2022

note: 我们已经看到可以发现脑肿瘤的头盔和人工智能，但一种新的安全帽实际上也可以治疗它们。作为最新神经学突破的一部分，研究人员使用头盔产生磁场将致命肿瘤缩小三分之一。接受治疗的 53 岁患者最终因无关的伤害去世。但是，对他的大脑进行的尸检显示，该手术在短时间内切除了 31% 的肿瘤块。该测试标志着第一种针对称为胶质母细胞瘤的致命脑癌的无创疗法。头盔的特点是三个旋转磁铁连接到一个由可充电电池操作的基于微处理器的电子控制器。作为治疗的一部分，患者在诊所佩戴该设备五周，然后在妻子的帮助下回家。头盔产生的磁场疗法最初进行了两个小时，然后逐渐增加到每天最多六个小时。在此期间，患者的肿瘤质量和体积缩小了近三分之一，缩小似乎与治疗剂量相关。该设备的发明者——获得了 FDA 的同情使用治疗的批准——声称它有朝一日可以帮助治疗脑癌，而无需放疗或化疗。 “我们的结果……开启了一个无创和无毒治疗的新世界……未来有许多令人兴奋的可能性，”Kenneth R. Peak 脑和垂体中心的通讯作者兼主任 David S. Baskin 说休斯顿卫理公会神经病学研究所神经外科的肿瘤治疗。该程序的详细信息已发表在同行评审的肿瘤学前沿杂志上。 Engadget 推荐的所有产品均由我们的编辑团队选择，独立于我们的母公司。我们的一些故事包括附属链接。如果您通过这些链接之一购买商品，我们可能会赚取会员佣金。由于您的隐私偏好，此内容不可用。在此处更新您的设置，然后重新加载页面以查看它。
note_en:
We've seen  helmets and  AI that can spot brain tumors, but a new hard hat can actually treat them, too. As part of the latest neurological  breakthrough, researchers used a helmet that generates a magnetic field to shrink a deadly tumor by a third. The 53-year-old patient who underwent the treatment ultimately passed away due to an unrelated injury. But, an autopsy of his brain showed that the procedure had removed 31 percent of the tumor mass in a short time. The test marked the first noninvasive therapy for a deadly form of brain cancer known as glioblastoma.
 The helmet features three rotating magnets connected to a microprocessor-based electronic controller operated by a rechargeable battery. As part of the therapy, the patient wore the device for five weeks at a clinic and then at home with the help of his wife. The resulting magnetic field therapy created by the helmet was administered for two hours initially and then ramped up to a maximum of six hours per day. During the period, the patient's tumor mass and volume shrunk by nearly a third, with shrinkage appearing to correlate with the treatment dose.
  The inventors of the device — which received FDA approval for compassionate use treatment — claim it could one day help treat brain cancer without radiation or chemotherapy. “Our results...open a new world of non-invasive and nontoxic therapy...with many exciting possibilities for the future,” said David S. Baskin, corresponding author and director of the Kenneth R. Peak Center for Brain and Pituitary Tumor Treatment in the Department of Neurosurgery at Houston Methodist Neurological Institute. Details of the procedure have been published in the peer-reviewed journal  Frontiers in Oncology.

title: 磁性头盔在世界首次测试中缩小了致命的肿瘤
title_en: A Magnetic Helmet Shrunk a Deadly Tumor In World-First Test

note: 本周早些时候，微软推出了新的云 PC Windows 365 服务，由于需求，该公司已经不得不暂停免费试用。 Windows 365 可让您租用云 PC（配备各种 CPU、RAM 和存储选项），然后通过 Web 浏览器流式传输 Windows 10 或 Windows 11。该服务仅在注册一天后就达到了最大容量。 Microsoft 365 Twitter 帐户的声明中写道：“随着大量需求，我们已经达到了 Windows 365 试用的容量。” “我们看到了对 Windows 365 的令人难以置信的反响，需要在我们提供额外容量的同时暂停我们的免费试用计划，”Windows 365 计划管理总监 Scott Manchester 解释说。 Windows 365 目前仅适用于企业，如果该公司在免费试用一天后就遇到了容量问题，那么其需求显然超出了微软的预期。微软周一推出了这项服务，如果您已经拥有 Windows 10 许可证，则每个用户的定价为一个 vCPU、2GB 内存、64GB 存储和 12GB 带宽的每月 20 美元起。 Windows 365 有多种配置，包括高端配置，包括 8 个 vCPU、32GB 内存和 512GB 存储空间，每月 158 美元。 Windows365.com 上提供了所有不同的价格选项，但您现在需要注册免费试用，因为 Microsoft 已达到容量上限。
note_en:
Microsoft launched its new cloud PC Windows 365 service  earlier this week, and the company has already had to pause free trials due to demand.  Windows 365 lets you rent a cloud PC — with a variety of CPU, RAM, and storage options — and then stream Windows 10 or Windows 11 via a web browser. The service reached max capacity after only a day of signups.
 “Following significant demand, we have reached capacity for Windows 365 trials,” reads a statement from the Microsoft 365 Twitter account. “We have seen unbelievable response to Windows 365 and need to pause our free trial program while we provision additional capacity,”  explains Scott Manchester, director of Windows 365 program management.
   Windows 365 is only available to businesses right now, and there’s clearly been more demand than Microsoft had anticipated if the company has hit capacity issues after just a day of free trials. Microsoft launched the service on Monday, with per-user pricing that starts at $20 per month for one vCPU, 2GB of RAM, 64GB of storage, and 12GB of bandwidth if you already have a Windows 10 license.
 There are a variety of configurations for Windows 365, including one at the top end that includes eight vCPUs, 32GB of RAM, and 512GB of storage for $158 per month. All of the different price options are available at  Windows365.com, but you’ll now need to  register interest in a free trial as Microsoft has hit capacity.
title: 微软暂停其新 Windows 365 服务的免费试用，该服务允许用户在“大量需求”后通过网络浏览器流式传输 Windows 10 或 11
title_en: Microsoft pauses free trials of its new Windows 365 service, which lets users stream Windows 10 or 11 via web browser, after “significant demand”

黑客。虚假信息。监视。 CYBER 是 Motherboard 的播客，报道了互联网的阴暗面。根据 Motherboard 获得的一份谷歌内部文件，谷歌在 2018 年至 2020 年期间解雇了数十名员工，原因是他们滥用了对公司工具或数据的访问权限，其中一些员工可能面临访问谷歌用户或员工数据的指控。该文件提供了关于科技巨头运营中一个通常很微妙的部分的具体数据：调查公司自己的员工如何利用他们的职位来窃取、泄露或滥用他们可能访问的数据。内部人员滥用是整个科技行业的一个问题。 Motherboard 之前在 Facebook、Snapchat 和 MySpace 上发现了一些实例，在某些情况下，员工会利用他们的访问权来跟踪或以其他方式监视用户。该文件称，谷歌在 2020 年因安全相关问题解雇了 36 名员工。在针对员工的所有与安全相关的指控中，86% 包括对机密信息处理不当，例如将仅供内部使用的信息传输给外部各方。该文件显示，2020 年所有指控中有 10% 涉及滥用系统，其中可能包括违反谷歌自己的政策访问用户或员工数据、帮助他人访问该数据，或者修改或删除用户或员工数据。 2019 年，这一数字占所有安全指控的 13%。您是否知道有任何公司员工利用他们对用户数据的访问权限？我们很乐意听取您的意见。使用非工作电话或计算机，您可以通过 +44 20 8133 5190 上的 Signal、josephcox 上的 Wickr 或电子邮件 joseph.cox@vice.com 安全地联系 Joseph Cox。提供文件的人士告诉 Motherboard，谷歌在 2019 年解雇了 26 名员工，2018 年解雇了 18 名与安全事件相关的员工。主板允许此人匿名更坦率地谈论 Google 问题。该文件称，谷歌可以对数据处理不当的员工采取的其他措施包括警告、培训和辅导。
谷歌发言人在一份声明中告诉 Motherboard：“所提到的情况主要与对专有和敏感的公司信息或 IP 的不当访问或滥用有关。” “关于用户数据，我们通过多项行业领先的保护措施严格限制员工访问，包括：将用户数据的访问权限限制为必要的个人、要求提供访问此类数据的理由、在授予敏感数据访问权限之前进行多阶段审查，以及监控访问异常和违规行为，”声明补充说。 “无论是有意还是无意，违规的数量一直很低。每位员工每年都接受培训，我们调查所有指控，违规会导致纠正措施，包括解雇。我们在公开调查的数量和结果方面是透明的我们的员工，并制定了严格的流程来保护客户和用户数据免受任何内部或外部威胁。” Gawker 当时报道称，2010 年，谷歌解雇了工程师大卫·巴克斯代尔 (David Barksdale)，理由是他利用他作为技术小组成员的职位访问了四名未成年人的账户。报告补充说，巴克斯代尔访问了一名 15 岁男孩的谷歌语音通话记录，以及联系人列表和聊天记录，并解除了与一名与他断绝联系的青少年的联系。
note_en:
Hacking. Disinformation. Surveillance. CYBER is Motherboard's podcast and reporting on the dark underbelly of the internet.
  Google fired dozens of employees between 2018 and 2020 for abusing their access to the company's tools or data, with some workers potentially facing allegations of accessing Google user or employee data, according to an internal Google document obtained by Motherboard.
  The document provides concrete figures on an often delicate part of a tech giant's operations: investigations into how the company's own employees leverage their positions to steal, leak, or abuse data they may have access to. Insider abuse is a problem across the tech industry. Motherboard previously  uncovered instances at Facebook,  Snapchat,  and MySpace, with employees in some cases using their access to stalk or otherwise spy on users.
    The document says that Google terminated 36 employees in 2020 for security-related issues. Eighty-six percent of all security-related allegations against employees included mishandling of confidential information, such as the transfer of internal-only information to outside parties.
  Ten percent of all allegations in 2020 concerned misuse of systems, which can include accessing user or employee data in violation of Google's own policies, helping others to access that data, or modifying or deleting user or employee data, according to the document. In 2019, that figure was 13 percent of all security allegations.
 Do you know about any company employees leveraging their access to user data? We'd love to hear from you. Using a non-work phone or computer, you can contact Joseph Cox securely on Signal on +44 20 8133 5190, Wickr on josephcox, or email joseph.cox@vice.com.
  Google terminated 26 people in 2019 and 18 in 2018 related to security incidents, the person who provided the document told Motherboard. Motherboard granted the person anonymity to speak more candidly about Google issues. The document says that other measures Google can take with employees that mishandled data can include warnings, training, and coaching.
  A Google spokesperson told Motherboard in a statement: "The instances referred to mostly relate to inappropriate access to, or misuse of, proprietary and sensitive corporate information or IP."
  "Regarding user data, we tightly restrict employee access through a number of industry leading safeguards, including: limiting access to user data to necessary individuals, requiring a justification to access such data, multi-stage review before access is granted to sensitive data, and monitoring for access anomalies and violations," the statement added. "The number of violations, whether deliberate or inadvertent, is consistently low. Every employee gets training annually, we investigate all allegations, and violations result in corrective action up to and including termination. We are transparent in publicizing the number and outcome of our investigations to our employees and have strict processes in place to secure customer and user data from any internal or external threats."
  In 2010, Google fired engineer David Barksdale for leveraging his position as a member of a technical group to access the accounts of four minors,   Gawker  reported at the time. Barksdale accessed a 15-year-old boy's Google Voice call logs, as well as contact lists and chat transcripts and unblocked himself from a teen who had cut communications with him, the report added.
title: 内部文件：谷歌在 2018 年至 2020 年期间解雇了数十名工程师滥用他们对数据和工具的访问
title_en: Internal document: Google fired dozens of engineers between 2018 and 2020 for abusing their access to data and tools

当 Superhuman 首席执行官 Rahul Vohra 同意与前 Dropbox 首席财务官 Ajay Vashee 的风险投资公司 IVP 会面，讨论对他的电子邮件生产力初创公司的投资时，这位企业家有一个条件：没有团体演示。对于许多初创公司来说，在周一的会议上推介一家风险投资公司的合作伙伴关系是一种成人礼。 Vohra 说他会与每个合作伙伴会面，以一对一的方式谈论 Superhuman。 “我讨厌走进办公室，在会议室里说，'这是我的甲板，' 敲桌子说'rah rah rah'，这是我的愿景。我不是美国创始人，我是一个非常低调的英国人，个性明智，”沃拉说。 “那只是我的文化。” Vohra 的方式花了更多的时间——Vashee 承认 IVP 不太可能让它成为它看到的每家初创公司的标准——但它得到了回报，因为 Superhuman 已经筹集了由 IVP 牵头的 7500 万美元的 C 轮融资。据知情人士透露，该轮融资包括 Tiger Global 和 Discord 和 Dropbox 首席执行官的个人投资以及其他一批人，对 Superhuman 的估值为 8.25 亿美元。这种定制方法也是 Vohra 和联合创始人 Conrad Irwin 和 Vivek Sodera 将 Superhuman 构建成一个软件应用程序的典型方式，该软件应用程序的候补名单超过 450,000。 Superhuman 成立于 2014 年，从未正式上线；到目前为止，只有 Gmail 和 iOS 用户从该列表中删除，他们必须通过 30 分钟的实时入职培训，才能获得每月支付 30 美元的特权，以获得许多其他免费提供的服务、电子邮件。它的价格和礼宾服务让 Superhuman 很容易被硅谷精英视为一种小奢侈品。但它允许 Superhuman 谨慎地增长，主要是靠自己的收入，同时从反馈开始调整其工具（杀手级功能：键盘快捷键，可以轻松回复预先编写的消息或模板、缩放链接和日历保留）用户第一次探索产品的那一刻。 Superhuman 没有分享其用户总数（在 2020 年，福布斯估计 Superhuman 在该初创公司出现在下一个十亿美元初创公司名单时已达到至少 50,000）。该公司确实表示，迄今为止，这些客户已经发送了超过 1 亿条消息来“分类”超过 3.35 亿个对话；在过去两年中，超过一半的月度用户每天都在使用该应用程序（并且超过 60%，不包括周末）。 Vohra 补充说，58% 的用户每天在该应用程序上花费的时间超过 3 小时（根据 Adob​​e 的一项调查，与普通员工在工作日在电子邮件上花费的时间相当），并且平均向其他人发送了 3.4 个邀请，在六个月内将至少一个人转化为客户。 “他们有志于成为工作场所沟通和生产力的实际领导者，并获得用户许可，”Vashee 说。看到 Dropbox 如何从个人用户自下而上扩展到企业的相似之处，IVP 投资者也将 Vohra 与他的一些前同事联系起来； Dropbox 首席执行官 Drew Houston 和联合创始人 Arash Ferdowsi 都亲自投资，总裁 Timothy Young 也是如此。 Young 的假设：电子邮件仍然是许多员工开始一天工作的地方。但是，向远程工作的转变已经消除了花费数小时检查电子邮件以在办公室环境中看起来富有成效的剧院，从而提高了效率和“高度自以为是的工作流程”。
Vohra 说，尽管在 2019 年由 Andreessen Horowitz 领导的 B 系列筹集了 3300 万美元，但 Superhuman 在获得新资金之前仍然有 3000 万美元在银行里。这笔资金将立即投入到该初创公司为 Android 和 Outlook 用户开放服务的持续推动中，这些用户代表了其大部分候补名单和前两项功能请求。在明年推出之前，前十名左右的测试人员已经在尝试早期版本的 Superhuman 的 Outlook 兼容服务；据 Vohra 称，Superhuman 的人数目前约为 85 人，到今年年底将达到约 120 人，到 2022 年底将达到 200 多人。多达一半的员工正在招募新用户并观察他们； Superhuman 的 CEO 表示，大部分时间里，Superhuman 的增长已被其可用性最大化。这可能很快就会改变，因为现在约有 5% 到 10% 的新用户通过 Superhuman 网站上为精通技术的采用者提供的测试自助服务选项进入。 Discord 的首席执行官 Jason Citron 在 1 月份以这种方式加入，而 Vohra 并不知道，然后才承诺在 6 月份进行投资。当然，减轻候补名单上的压力有利于 Superhuman 的底线——并有助于消除它作为科技行业必备工具的任何内涵。该公司已经通过招聘新的高管来推动企业销售； Vohra 说，超过一半的 Superhuman 许可证已经由雇主直接或通过费用支付，企业客户的净保留率在 130% 到 180% 之间波动。但是 Superhuman 可以通过日历工具和与其他工作应用程序的集成做更多的事情；该公司还计划添加基本任务管理等功能以将电子邮件分配给同事，以及自动更正和其他写作辅助工具以加快撰写电子邮件的速度。 Superhuman 建立在已有数十年历史的电子邮件客户端技术之上，必须在不降低其体验的情况下完成所有这些工作，这也是一个技术难题。 Superhuman 现在还包括 Thrive Global 首席执行官 Arianna Huffington、Brex 联合首席执行官 Henrique Dubugras、演员 Ashton Kutcher、Mila Kunis 和 Will Smith、音乐团体 The Chainsmokers 和一些职业运动员，其人数超过 150 人。作为一名狂热的投资者，Vohra 说他们可以帮助 Superhuman 扩展到新行业——Vohra 正在与赫芬顿合作，探讨如何将 Superhuman 纳入 Thrive 以健康为导向的公司关系——但也允许 Superhuman 团队向同样致力于效率和卓越的领导者学习在自己的领域。让电子邮件变得更少是 Superhuman 今天的既定使命。它远不是唯一一家解决这个问题的公司。 Vohra 本人之前曾将一家电子邮件初创公司 Rapportive 出售给了 LinkedIn，而 Dropbox 收购了另一家 Mailbox，该公司后来倒闭了。 Vohra 的希望是 Superhuman 有机会通过逐渐成长，然后突然“跨越鸿沟”走出湾区和科技行业，从协作应用程序 Slack 的剧本中汲取一页。用一只眼睛，Vohra 已经在关注 Superhuman 在消息应用程序和像 Slack 这样的实时通信方面的未来。 “我认为我们不会永远是一家电子邮件公司，”他说。
note_en:
When Superhuman CEO Rahul Vohra agreed to meet with former Dropbox CFO Ajay Vashee’s venture capital firm IVP about an investment in his email productivity startup, the entrepreneur had one condition: no group presentation. For many startups, pitching a VC firm’s partnership during its Monday meetings is a rite of passage. Vohra said he’d meet with each and every partner to talk about Superhuman one-on-one instead.
   “I hate coming into the office, being in a boardroom and saying, ‘Here’s my deck,’ pounding the table about ‘rah rah rah,’ my vision. I’m not an American founder, I’m a very understated British type, personality wise,” Vohra says. “That’s just my culture.”
 Vohra’s way took many more hours – Vashee admits IVP is unlikely to make it standard for every startup it sees – but it paid off, as Superhuman has raised a $75 million Series C funding round led by IVP. The round, which included Tiger Global and personal investments from the CEOs of Discord and Dropbox and a bevy of others, values Superhuman at $825 million, according to a source with knowledge of the transaction.
 That bespoke approach is also typical of how Vohra and cofounders Conrad Irwin and Vivek Sodera have built Superhuman into a software app with a waitlist of more than 450,000. Founded in 2014, Superhuman has never formally launched; customers, Gmail and iOS users only so far, who make it off that list must go through a 30-minute live onboarding session for the privilege to pay $30 per month for a service, email, offered by many others for free.
 Its price point and concierge service makes Superhuman easy to dismiss as a small luxury for the Silicon Valley elite. But it’s allowed Superhuman to grow cautiously, largely off its own revenue, all while tweaking its tools (the killer feature: keyboard shortcuts that can make it easy to reply with pre-written messages or templates, Zoom links and calendar holds) from feedback starting the moment a user first explores the product.
  Superhuman doesn’t share its total number of users (in 2020,  Forbes estimated Superhuman had reached at least 50,000 when the startup appeared on the list of  Next Billion Dollar Startups). The company does say those customer have sent more than 100 million messages to “triage” more than 335 million conversations to date; more than half of monthly users have worked in the app daily for the past two years (and more than 60% excluding weekends). Vohra adds that 58% of users spend more than 3 hours per day in the app (on par with what the average worker already spends on email in a weekday,  per an Adobe survey), and have sent an average of 3.4 invites to others, converting at least one person into a customer, within six months.
 “They have the ambition, as well as the user permission, to become a de facto leader in workplace communication and productivity,” says Vashee. Seeing parallels to how Dropbox spread bottoms-up from individual users into businesses, the IVP investor connected Vohra to some of his former colleagues, too; Dropbox CEO Drew Houston and cofounder Arash Ferdowsi both personally invested, as did president Timothy Young. Young’s hypothesis: email is still where many workers start their day. But the shift to remote work has stripped away the theater of spending hours checking email to look productive in an office setting, placing a premium on efficiency and “hyper-opinionated workflows.”
   Despite raising $33 million in a Series B led by Andreessen Horowitz  in 2019, Superhuman still had $30 million in the bank ahead of its new funding, Vohra says. The money will be poured immediately into the startup’s ongoing push to open up its service for Android and Outlook users, who represent much of its waitlist and its top two feature requests. The first ten or so testers are already trying an early version of Superhuman’s Outlook-compatible service ahead of its availability next year; Superhuman’s head count, today around 85, will reach about 120 by end of year and 200-plus by the end of 2022, according to Vohra.
 Up to about half of those staff are onboarding new users and observing them; most weeks, Superhuman’s growth has been maxed out by their availability, its CEO says. That could be changing soon, as about 5% to 10% of new users are now coming in through a test self-service option on Superhuman’s site for tech-savvy adopters. Discord CEO Jason Citron came in that way in January, unbeknownst to Vohra, before committing to invest in June.
 Alleviating the pressure on its waitlist would, of course, be good for Superhuman’s bottom line—and help eliminate any connotation it has as a nice-to-have tool for the tech industry. Already, the company is pushing into enterprise sales with new executive hires; more than half of Superhuman licenses are already paid for by employers directly or through expensing, Vohra says, with net retention of corporate customers fluctuating between 130% and 180%.
 But Superhuman could do more with calendar tools and integrations with other work apps; the company also plans to add features like basic task management to assign emails to colleagues, as well as auto-correct and other writing assistance tools to make composing emails faster. Built on decades-old email client technology, Superhuman must do all that without slowing down its experience, too, a technical puzzle.
  Superhuman now also counts Thrive Global CEO Arianna Huffington, Brex co-CEO Henrique Dubugras, actors Ashton Kutcher, Mila Kunis and Will Smith, music group The Chainsmokers and some professional athletes among its cap table of more than 150 individuals. An  avid investor himself, Vohra says they can help Superhuman expand into new industries—Vohra is working with Huffington about ways to incorporate Superhuman into Thrive’s wellness-oriented corporate relationships—but also allows the Superhuman team to learn from leaders similarly committed to efficiency and excellence in their own fields.
 Making email suck less is Superhuman’s stated mission today. It’s  far from the only company to tackle that problem; Vohra himself previously sold an email startup, Rapportive, to LinkedIn, while Dropbox acquired another, Mailbox,  that later withered, among others. Vohra’s hope is that Superhuman has a shot to take a page out of collaboration app Slack’s playbook by growing gradually, then suddenly, “crossing the chasm” out of the Bay Area and tech industry.
 With one eye, Vohra’s already looking at Superhuman’s future in messaging apps and real-time communications like Slack. “I don’t think we will remain an email company forever,” he says.
title: 电子邮件服务 Superhuman 每月收费 30 美元，尚未正式推出，在 IVP 领投的 C 轮融资中筹集了 7500 万美元，消息人士称估值为 8.25 亿美元
title_en: Email service Superhuman, which charges $30/month and hasn't formally launched, raises $75M Series C led by IVP, source says at a $825M valuation

Instacart 表示，埃弗森将负责监管多个部门，包括广告、合作伙伴关系、政策和法律部门。埃弗森在 Facebook 工作了 10 多年，在那里她担任该公司全球业务集团副总裁的主要广告商代言人。杂货配送应用程序 Instacart 周三宣布，它已经聘请了另一位前 Facebook 高管：长期担任 Facebook 广告主管的卡罗琳·埃弗森 (Carolyn Everson)，她于 6 月离开了该公司。在 Facebook 应用程序前负责人 Fidji Simo 接任首席执行官仅几周后，埃弗森就搬到了 Instacart。 Instacart 是剩余的私营快递公司中最大的一家，但面临着来自 Uber 不断增长的快递业务以及 GoPuff 等新贵的竞争。在 3 月份的一轮融资中，它的估值为 390 亿美元，人们普遍预计它将在明年上市。 Everson 将于 9 月 7 日加入 Instacart 担任总裁一职，接替现任总裁 Nilam Ganenthiran，后者将成为 CEO 的战略顾问。 Instacart 表示，埃弗森将负责多个部门，包括广告、合作伙伴关系、政策和法律部门。埃弗森在 Facebook 工作了 10 多年，在那里她担任该公司全球业务集团副总裁的主要广告商代言人。在周三接受 CNBC 的“TechCheck”采访时，Simo 谈到了为什么 Instacart 对 Facebook 的顶尖人才如此有吸引力。她说，该公司从 Facebook、谷歌和亚马逊引进了“同等数量”的员工。
“我认为不仅要谈​​论性别和种族的多样性，还要谈论思想的多样性，这真的很重要，因此吸引来自非常不同的公司、拥有非常不同的经历的人对我们来说非常重要，”西莫说。 “我想你知道 Carolyn 和我在 Facebook 加入 Instacart 10 年后非常兴奋，这说明了业务的实力和 Instacart 面前的机会。” Facebook 首席多元化官 Maxine Williams 在 7 月表示，Simo 和 Everson 的离职并不是一件坏事，因为他们正在其他地方担任高级领导职务。 Instacart 表示，随着 Everson 的加入，其执行领导团队的 55% 现在是女性。
note_en:
Everson will oversee a plethora of divisions including advertising, partnerships, policy and legal, Instacart said.
 Everson spent more than 10 years at Facebook where she served as the face of the company for major advertisers as the vice president of its global business group.
 Grocery delivery app Instacart on Wednesday announced it has nabbed yet another former  Facebook executive: Longtime Facebook ads chief Carolyn Everson, who  left the company in June. Everson is moving to Instacart mere weeks after Fidji Simo, former head of the Facebook app,  took over as CEO.
 Instacart is the largest of the remaining privately held delivery companies, but faces competition from  Uber's growing delivery business as well as upstarts like GoPuff. It was  valued at $39 billion in a March funding round and is widely expected to go public in the next year.
  Everson will join Instacart on Sept. 7 in the role of president, succeeding current President Nilam Ganenthiran, who will become a strategic advisor to the CEO. Everson will oversee several divisions, including advertising, partnerships, policy and legal, Instacart said.
 Everson spent more than 10 years at Facebook where she served as the face of the company for major advertisers as the vice president of its global business group.
 In an interview with CNBC's "TechCheck," Wednesday, Simo addressed why Instacart is becoming so appealing to top Facebook talent. She said the company has brought in "an equal number" of workers from Facebook,  Google and  Amazon.
 "I think it's really important to talk not just about diversity of gender and race but also diversity of thoughts, so it's really important for us to attract people from very different companies that have very different experiences," Simo said. "And I think you know the fact that Carolyn and I were so excited after 10 years at Facebook to join Instacart, just speaks to the strength of the business and the opportunities that Instacart has in front of it."
 Facebook Chief Diversity Officer  Maxine Williams in July said the departures of Simo and Everson are not such a bad thing because they're moving into high-ranking leadership roles elsewhere.
  Instacart said with the addition of Everson, 55% of its executive leadership team is now female.
title: Instacart 聘请了长期担任 Facebook 广告主管的卡罗琳·埃弗森 (Carolyn Everson)，后者于 9 月 7 日开始担任总裁一职，此前 Facebook 还聘请了其他知名人士，后者于 6 月离职
title_en: Instacart hires longtime Facebook ads chief Carolyn Everson, who left in June, as president, starting Sept. 7, following other high-profile hires from Facebook
